input.command:
cd /app; nohup qemu-system-x86_64 -m 1024 -smp 2 -cdrom /app/alpine.iso -boot d -display none -serial telnet:127.0.0.1:6665,server,nowait -monitor none > /tmp/qemu.log 2>&1 &
echo "pid=$!"; sleep 3; echo "---log---"; head -40 /tmp/qemu.log; echo "---port---"; (exec 3<>/dev/tcp/127.0.0.1/6665) 2>&1 && echo "port open" || echo "port closed"

cat > /tmp/probe.py <<'EOF'
import socket,sys,time
def probe():
    s=socket.socket(); s.settimeout(6)
    try: s.connect(('127.0.0.1',6665))
    except Exception as e: return "connfail:%s"%e
    try: s.sendall(b"\r\n")
    except: pass
    time.sleep(1.5)
    data=b""
    s.settimeout(3)
    try:
        while True:
            d=s.recv(4096)
            if not d: break
            data+=d
    except: pass
    s.close()
    # strip telnet IAC negotiation bytes crudely
    return data
d=probe()
if isinstance(d,bytes):
    txt=d.replace(b'\xff',b' ')
    sys.stdout.buffer.write(txt[-600:])
    print("\n[LOGIN]" ,"login:" in d.decode('latin1').lower())
else:
    print(d)
EOF
python3 /tmp/probe.py
sleep 60; python3 /tmp/probe.py; echo "=== qemu alive? ==="; ps -p 6305 -o pid,etime,cmd 2>/dev/null | tail -1
ps aux | grep -w qemu-system-x86_64 | grep -v grep | head; echo "---qemu.log size---"; ls -l /tmp/qemu.log; wc -c /tmp/qemu.log

content.text:
Ready. Alpine 3.19 booted under QEMU; `telnet 127.0.0.1 6665` presents `localhost login:` (getty on `/dev/ttyS0`).

- QEMU pid 6305, running in background (`-serial telnet:127.0.0.1:6665,server,nowait`, no KVM → TCG, boot took ~2min).
- Verified: connected to 6665, received `localhost login:` prompt.
- getty respawns → each new telnet connection gets a fresh prompt.

Verification: probe script connected to port 6665, read `Welcome to Alpine Linux 3.19 … localhost login:` → `[LOGIN] True`.
